repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem:
`K` and `CTRL-]` in help files may fail if it's in the middle of some noisy text. Example:

      (`fun(config: vim.lsp.ClientConfig): boolean`)
                            ^cursor

## Solution:

- Implement a DWIM heuristic: start with `<cWORD>` and trim punctuation until a valid tag is found.
- `:help!` (bang, no args) activates DWIM behavior.
- Set `keywordprg=:help!` by default.
- Does not affect `CTRL-]`, that is still fully "tags" based.



### TODO (future):

- `:help {subject}` should handle more of these cases. https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/38308

cc @brianhuster @yochem might have comments on this

comment_data.body: Default to `:help!` so that DWIM `K` is enabled by default in help files.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -17,7 +17,7 @@ let b:undo_ftplugin = "setl isk< fo< tw< cole< cocu< keywordprg< omnifunc< comme
 
 setl comments= cms=
 
-setlocal formatoptions+=tcroql textwidth=78 keywordprg=:help omnifunc=s:HelpComplete
+setlocal formatoptions+=tcroql textwidth=78 keywordprg=:help! omnifunc=s:HelpComplete

comment_data.body: I hand-crafted this em-dash I swear 🦞
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -128,6 +128,201 @@ function M.escape_subject(word)
   return word
 end
 
+--- Characters that are considered punctuation for trimming help tags.
+--- Dots (.) are NOT included here — they're trimmed separately as a last resort.

@@ -128,6 +128,201 @@ function M.escape_subject(word)
   return word
 end
 
+--- Characters that are considered punctuation for trimming help tags.
+--- Dots (.) are NOT included here — they're trimmed separately as a last resort.
+local trimmable_punct = {

comment_regular_data.body:
Definitely nice to have help "try as much as possible" to find a tag similar to the word under the cursor. If you really want to do this without remapping `K`, I've found that this hacky approach works:

```lua
local function _G.help_keywordprg(word)
  M.open_helptag()
end

vim.bo.keywordprog = [[:lua help_keywordprg()--]]
```

But wouldn't it be easier to have `:help` try `<cWORD>` if the "search term" didn't match and the cursor is currently in a help buffer?

> "K" (or `CTRL-]`) in help files 

I'm unsure if I have interpretted this correctly, but the failure happens outside of help files as well. For example, inside of an LSP hover. Does this change handle that case as well?

My own hacky solution to this issue was to just overwrite `K` with this function:

```
function Help_lookup()
    local expr = vim.fn.expand('<cexpr>')
    if pcall(vim.cmd, 'help ' .. expr) then
        return
    end

    local word = vim.fn.expand('<cword>')
    -- Remove trailing non-alpha
    word = word:gsub("[^%a]+$", "")
    if pcall(vim.cmd, 'help ' .. word) then
        return
    end

    vim.lsp.buf.hover()
    print('No help/LSP entry: ' .. expr)
end
```

I haven't had this solution miss anything, but your iterative approach might catch some edge cases.

I include the LSP hover as the last resort because it's nice having a universal keybind for "Show me the documentation of this thing", but wouldn't think that's good for core.

> have `:help` try `<cWORD>`

I'll do it that way. Then this can be driven by `keywordprg=:help` without mapping `K`.
I haven't tried this, but it seems like this could also replace #32995, right?

With #37757 merged, to make ``(`vim.lsp.ClientConfig`)`` work would require the following one line change:

```diff
diff --git i/runtime/lua/vim/_core/help.lua w/runtime/lua/vim/_core/help.lua
index a8c16305f1..ac49f348aa 100644
--- i/runtime/lua/vim/_core/help.lua
+++ w/runtime/lua/vim/_core/help.lua
@@ -122,7 +122,7 @@ function M.escape_subject(word)
     -- E.g. '`command`,' --> 'command' (backticks are removed too, but '``' stays '``')
     word = word:gsub([[^'([^']*)'.*]], [['%1']])
     word = word:gsub([[^{([^}]*)}.*]], '{%1}')
-    word = word:gsub([[^`([^`]+)`.*]], '%1')
+    word = word:gsub([[.*`([^`]+)`.*]], '%1')
   end

   return word
```

But that does not work yet for ``(`lsp.clientCapabilities`)``, as that would require to construct a pattern like `*lsp.clientCapabilities` to account for the missing `vim.`.

See updated PR description.

This is working pretty nicely. The Lua code probably can use cleanup, but it's isolated so not a blocker.

Will merge this soon if no comments.
This function seems to share quite a bit of functionality with `vim._core.help.escape_subject()`: cleaning noise around a potential help tag. Can't that behavior be merged? Or would that break stuff?

> This function seems to share quite a bit of functionality with `vim._core.help.escape_subject()`: cleaning noise around a potential help tag. Can't that behavior be merged?

Very likely. Don't want that to  block this, help is welcome though. But (correct me if  wrong), isn't the "escaping" logic intended workaround the lack of `:nospecial` https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/37862 ? 

Also, `:help {subject}` (without "!") should be able to handle "sloppy" `{subject}` similar to how `:help!` works in this PR. Will track that in a backlog issue https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/38308. (Note: `:help!` benefits from knowing the cursor position, so will still have a small advantage.)

> Very likely. Don't want that to block this, help is welcome though. But (correct me if wrong), isn't the "escaping" logic intended workaround the lack of :nospecial https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/37862 ?

Yes, most of it. But `:nospecial` would probably not be able to convert `:help g*` to `:help gstar`, which is needed because the helptag is `*gstar*`.

I understand not wanting to block this. For https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/38308 we could probably check if `:help {subject}` works with `vim._core.help.trim_tag` (from this PR) as "backend". Backlog is fine 👍🏼

title: feat(help): super K (`:help!`) guesses tag at cursor
